commits.role: shared verification-outcome vocabulary module with focused tests
classified index and registry audits, outcome fixtures, documentation, dead-duplicate removal

files.role: consumer documentation and registry scope note

interfaces: ai_lab.documentation.verification_outcome (rollup_outcome, rollup_from_findings, ok_from_outcome, outcome and class constants)
limits: The registry audit performs no git-evidence verification, so it reports only verified_current or stale; an unverifiable registry outcome would require an evidence-dependent registry check that does not exist.
The compact artifact sidecar contract (PLAN-20260717-0001) has not yet adopted the vocabulary; cross-subsystem harmonization completes only when its implementation imports verification_outcome.

Consumers gating on error counts by finding code rather than the ok or verification_outcome fields will observe reduced error counts in evidence-free environments; this is the documented intentional severity reclassification.
name: Three-valued verification outcome vocabulary in self-model audits
recommended_next_actions: Implement the admitted sidecar contract (PLAN-20260717-0001) importing verification_outcome for its staleness semantics, then close GAP-0004 with a verification confirming both subsystems share one vocabulary.
risks: If a future audit emits findings without a class field, rollup treats them as other and they cannot influence the outcome; new emission sites must classify explicitly.
Divergence risk persists until the sidecar implementation consumes the shared module, tracked by keeping GAP-0004 open.
summary: The self-model index and registry audits distinguish confirmed drift from unavailable evidence using a shared three-valued verification_outcome vocabulary (verified_current, stale, unverifiable) defined in ai_lab/documentation/verification_outcome.py, which imports from neither the self-model module nor any sidecar implementation. Every finding carries a class (drift, evidence_unavailable, other); evidence-unavailable findings carry warn severity with an evidence_status cause (no_git_dir, unreachable_repo_head); content-stale and repo-head-missing classification is deterministic from git-evidence availability captured at emission; rollup is deterministic with stale dominating unverifiable dominating verified_current; the ok field remains boolean and fail-closed, true only for verified_current.
